parameters.name: category
parameters.description:
* `--` - --
* `policy` - Policy
* `process` - Process
* `technical` - Technical
* `physical` - Physical
* `procedure` - Procedure

parameters.name: control_impact
parameters.description:
* `--` - --
* `1` - Very Low
* `2` - Low
* `3` - Medium
* `4` - High
* `5` - Very High

parameters.name: csf_function
parameters.description:
* `--` - --
* `govern` - Govern
* `identify` - Identify
* `protect` - Protect
* `detect` - Detect
* `respond` - Respond
* `recover` - Recover

parameters.name: effort
parameters.description:
Relative effort of the measure (using T-Shirt sizing)

* `--` - --
* `XS` - Extra Small
* `S` - Small
* `M` - Medium
* `L` - Large
* `XL` - Extra Large

post.description:
Perform a batch action on multiple objects.
Uses the IAM-filtered queryset and serializers to respect permissions
and validation, mirroring the standard partial_update / destroy flows.

Payload: { "action": "delete"|"change_field"|"change_m2m"|"add_m2m"|"remove_m2m"|"change_folder",
           "ids": [...], "field": "<field_name>", "value": ... }

Batch create multiple assets from a text list with parent-child relationships.
Expected format:
{
    "assets_text": "PR:Parent Asset\n  SP:Child Asset 1\n  SP:Child Asset 2",
    "folder": "folder-uuid"
}
Lines with leading spaces (2+) are children of the previous non-indented line.

get.description:
Requirement tree with domain-tree inheritance applied as an overlay.

Same shape as ``tree`` but each node also carries an ``inheritance``
key when an ancestor audit (same framework, up the Folder tree) covers
that requirement. The overlay holds the effective result/score, the
winning source audit, and the full inheritance path. The combination
strategy is the org-wide ``audit_tree_aggregation_strategy`` setting;
when it is ``none`` the overlay map is empty and this behaves like
``tree``.

parameters.name: termination_reason
parameters.description:
Reason for contract termination

* `eba_CO:x4` - Termination not for cause: Expired and not renewed
* `eba_CO:x5` - Termination for cause: Provider in breach of applicable law, regulations or contractual provisions
* `eba_CO:x6` - Termination for cause: Identified impediments of the provider capable of altering the supported function
* `eba_CO:x7` - Termination for cause: Provider's weaknesses regarding the management and security of sensitive data or information
* `eba_CO:x8` - Termination: As requested by the competent authority
* `eba_CO:x9` - Other reasons for termination

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A UUID string identifying this Quantitative Risk Study.

get.description:
Returns executive summary data for the quantitative risk study.
Includes scenarios that are selected and not in draft status with:
- Main information (ref_id, name, description)
- Assets, threats, qualifications links
- LEC chart data (current, selected residual, risk tolerance)
- Current and residual ALE insights
- Treatment cost of selected residual hypothesis

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A UUID string identifying this Quantitative Risk Study.

get.description:
Returns key metrics data for quantitative risk scenarios scoped per study.
Provides the following info per scenario based on risk metrics:
- name
- ale (Annual Loss Expectancy)
- var_95 (Value at Risk 95%)
- var_99 (Value at Risk 99%)
- var_999 (Value at Risk 99.9%)
- proba_of_exceeding_threshold (Probability of exceeding the loss threshold)

Data is provided for both current and residual risk levels based on risk_stage.
Current level uses hypothesis with risk_stage='current'
Residual level uses hypothesis with risk_stage='residual' and is_selected=True

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A UUID string identifying this Quantitative Risk Study.

post.description:
Retriggers all simulations for the quantitative risk study.
This includes:
- All hypothesis simulations (for each scenario's hypotheses with valid parameters)
- Portfolio simulation (combined ALE and LEC curves)
- Risk tolerance curve generation

This operation can be slow as it processes multiple simulations.

post.description:
Perform a batch action on multiple objects.
Uses the IAM-filtered queryset and serializers to respect permissions
and validation, mirroring the standard partial_update / destroy flows.

Payload: { "action": "delete"|"change_field"|"change_m2m"|"add_m2m"|"remove_m2m"|"change_folder",
           "ids": [...], "field": "<field_name>", "value": ... }

Batch create multiple feared events from a text list.
Expected format:
{
    "feared_events_text": "Feared Event 1\nFeared Event 2\nREF-001:Feared Event 3",
    "ebios_rm_study": "study-uuid"
}
Lines can optionally have a ref_id prefix (REF-001:Feared Event Name).
Feared events with the same name in the study will be skipped.

delete.description:
Convert Django's ProtectedError into a 409 Conflict with the list of
blocking references, so the frontend can render "this entity is used
as a subcontractor/recipient in N solutions" rather than a default 500.

Both the subcontractor and recipient FKs on SolutionSubcontractor use
on_delete=PROTECT, so deleting an Entity referenced by either role
raises ProtectedError. Collect blocking rows for both roles.

Batch create multiple entities from a text list.
Expected format:
{
    "entities_text": "Entity 1\nEntity 2\nREF-001:Entity 3",
    "folder": "folder-uuid"
}
Lines can optionally have a ref_id prefix (REF-001:Entity Name).
Entities with the same name in the folder will be skipped.

API endpoint that allows entities to be viewed or edited.

Generate DORA Register of Information (ROI) as a zip file containing CSV data.

This generates a comprehensive DORA ROI export containing multiple CSV reports:
- b_01.01: Main entity information
- b_01.02: Entity register (main entity + branches)
- b_01.03: Branches register
- b_02.01-03: Contractual arrangements
- b_03.01-03: Signing entities and providers
- b_04.01: Entities using ICT services
- b_05.01-02: Provider details and supply chains
- b_06.01: Critical functions register
- b_07.01: Assessment of ICT services
- b_99.01: Aggregation report
- FilingIndicators.csv: Template inclusion indicators
- parameters.csv: Report metadata and configuration
- META-INF/reportPackage.json: XBRL report package metadata
- reports/report.json: XBRL CSV configuration and taxonomy references

Generate graph data for entities, showing:
- Entity hierarchy (parent-child relationships)
- Solutions provided by entities
- Contracts between entities
- Assets linked to solutions
- Asset hierarchy (parent-child asset relationships)
Override the list method to inject optimized data into the serializer context.

post.description:
Perform a batch action on multiple objects.
Uses the IAM-filtered queryset and serializers to respect permissions
and validation, mirroring the standard partial_update / destroy flows.

Payload: { "action": "delete"|"change_field"|"change_m2m"|"add_m2m"|"remove_m2m"|"change_folder",
           "ids": [...], "field": "<field_name>", "value": ... }

Bulk-upload evidences from a multipart payload.

Form fields:
    folder (UUID, required): target folder.
    conflict_strategy (str, default 'skip'): one of skip / add_revision / replace / rename.
    manifest (JSON string, required): list of {"field": "file_0", "name": "...", "rel_path": "..."}.
    file_0 ... file_N: the actual file blobs, keyed by the manifest's "field" entries.

Returns:
    { "summary": {...counts...}, "results": [...per-file outcomes...] }

get.description:
Cross-assessment report for one framework.

Returns a flat list of RequirementAssessment rows across every
ComplianceAssessment using this framework that the user can read,
plus framework scoring metadata. Redacted fields (per CA's
field_visibility for the viewer's role) come back as null so the
row still contributes to domain/section counts.

Only "live" CAs participate by default: status in (in_progress,
in_review, done). This excludes planned (not yet started) and
deprecated (superseded by a newer audit on the same folder),
which would otherwise double-count requirements when multiple
audit cycles coexist on the same folder.

Query params:
  - implementation_group (str): narrow to a single IG.
    Default honors each CA's selected_implementation_groups.

post.description:
POST endpoint for atomic database + attachments restore.
Accepts multipart form data with:
- backup: database backup file (required)
- attachments: streaming binary data in custom format (optional)

This ensures both operations happen in a single authenticated request,
avoiding token invalidation issues.

get.description: Returns the configured default language. Falls back to English if unset or invalid.

Return a {dashboard_uuid: dashboard_name} map of dashboards
the requester can see, so an admin can pick one as the
instance default for the analytics Custom tab.

post.description:
Partial update of the default_custom_analytics_dashboard key.

Guarded by change_globalsettings so non-admins can't change the
org-wide default from the analytics Custom tab.

Note: this bypasses GeneralSettingsSerializer.update on purpose
because that serializer replaces the entire `value` dict with the
payload (no real PATCH semantics). For this single-key change we
merge into the existing dict. If the general settings ever grow
cross-key side effects beyond currency propagation, route this
through the serializer instead.

parameters.name: dora_alternative_providers_identified
parameters.description:
Are there alternative ICT third-party service providers identified?

* `eba_BT:x28` - Yes
* `eba_BT:x29` - No
* `eba_BT:x21` - Assessment not performed

parameters.name: dora_data_sensitiveness
parameters.description:
Sensitiveness of the data stored

* `eba_ZZ:x791` - Low
* `eba_ZZ:x792` - Medium
* `eba_ZZ:x793` - High

parameters.name: dora_discontinuing_impact
parameters.description:
Impact of discontinuing the ICT services

* `eba_ZZ:x791` - Low
* `eba_ZZ:x792` - Medium
* `eba_ZZ:x793` - High
* `eba_ZZ:x799` - Assessment not performed

parameters.name: dora_has_exit_plan
parameters.description:
Existence of an exit plan

* `eba_BT:x28` - Yes
* `eba_BT:x29` - No

parameters.name: dora_ict_service_type
parameters.description:
DORA ICT service type classification

* `eba_TA:S01` - ICT project management
* `eba_TA:S02` - ICT Development
* `eba_TA:S03` - ICT help desk and first level support
* `eba_TA:S04` - ICT security management services
* `eba_TA:S05` - Provision of data
* `eba_TA:S06` - Data analysis
* `eba_TA:S07` - ICT, facilities and hosting services (excluding Cloud services)
* `eba_TA:S08` - Computation
* `eba_TA:S09` - Non-Cloud Data storage
* `eba_TA:S10` - Telecom carrier
* `eba_TA:S11` - Network infrastructure
* `eba_TA:S12` - Hardware and physical devices
* `eba_TA:S13` - Software licencing (excluding SaaS)
* `eba_TA:S14` - ICT operation management (including maintenance)
* `eba_TA:S15` - ICT Consulting
* `eba_TA:S16` - ICT Risk management
* `eba_TA:S17` - Cloud services: IaaS
* `eba_TA:S18` - Cloud services: PaaS
* `eba_TA:S19` - Cloud services: SaaS

parameters.name: dora_non_substitutability_reason
parameters.description:
Reason if the ICT third-party service provider is considered not substitutable or difficult to be substitutable

* `eba_ZZ:x963` - Lack of real alternatives
* `eba_ZZ:x964` - Difficulties in migrating or reintegrating
* `eba_ZZ:x965` - Lack of real alternatives and difficulties in migrating or reintegrating

parameters.name: dora_reintegration_possibility
parameters.description:
Possibility of reintegration of the contracted ICT service

* `eba_ZZ:x798` - Easy
* `eba_ZZ:x966` - Difficult
* `eba_ZZ:x967` - Highly complex
* `eba_ZZ:x0` - Not applicable

parameters.name: dora_reliance_level
parameters.description:
Level of reliance on the ICT service

* `eba_ZZ:x794` - Not significant
* `eba_ZZ:x795` - Low reliance
* `eba_ZZ:x796` - Material reliance
* `eba_ZZ:x797` - Full reliance

parameters.name: dora_substitutability
parameters.description:
Substitutability of the ICT third-party service provider

* `eba_ZZ:x959` - Not substitutable
* `eba_ZZ:x962` - Easily substitutable
* `eba_ZZ:x961` - Medium complexity in terms of substitutability
* `eba_ZZ:x960` - Highly complex substitutability

POST endpoint for atomic database + attachments restore.

avoiding token invalidation issues.

AgentActionWrite.description:
Same defence-in-depth as AgentRunWriteSerializer. Agent actions are
AI proposals: the worker creates them, users only flip ``state`` via
approve/reject, and the rest of the row is immutable audit data.

config.description: Per-run scratch space for mode flags, source pointers, and other kind-specific knobs (e.g. {mode: 'external', ingest_namespace: 'agentrun:<uuid>'}). Read by the worker, not exposed to clients for direct write.

chat_session.description: Optional — only set when the run uses chat (e.g. ask_user).

AgentRunWrite.description:
Belt-and-braces: generic create/update on AgentRun is already 405'd at
the ViewSet, but if a future contributor re-enables it, the writable
surface here is locked down so a client can never aim a run at an
arbitrary object or stuff worker-owned counters.

AppliedControlList.description:
Lightweight serializer for the applied controls list view.

Drops the per-row DB-touching fields from `AppliedControlReadSerializer`
that the list table does not render:
  - `findings_count` (source="findings.count" → COUNT per row)
  - `ranking_score` (iterates non-prefetched risk_scenarios → 1 query per row)
  - `annual_cost` / `annual_cost_display` / `currency`
    (property hits GlobalSettings per row, called twice)
  - `state`, `evidences`, `objectives`, `security_exceptions`, `path`
    (not displayed on the table)

Inherits from `BaseModelSerializer` (not `AppliedControlWriteSerializer`)
to skip the unconditional `SyncMapping` query in Write.to_representation
that would otherwise also fire per row on the list.

AssetList.description:
Lightweight serializer for the assets list view.

Only includes fields rendered in the assets table (see frontend `table.ts`),
plus the aggregated objectives that the list also displays. Capability
aggregates and the `*_comparison` fields are skipped here to avoid the
per-row graph traversals they trigger — those remain on `AssetReadSerializer`
for the detail view.

AuditSink.description:
Serializer for audit-sink endpoints (kind=AUDIT_SINK): admin-managed
destinations that forward the audit log to an external SIEM. No HMAC secret
or per-event subscription — the whole audit feed is forwarded in the chosen
body_format, authenticated via static headers.

field_visibility.description: Per-field visibility map: {field_name: {role: 'edit' | 'read' | 'hidden'}}. Missing keys cascade through core.utils.DEFAULT_VISIBILITY (e.g. score/documentation_score default to hidden, status/extended_result to auditor-only) and finally to 'edit' for every role for unknown fields.

termination_reason.description:
Reason for contract termination

* `eba_CO:x4` - Termination not for cause: Expired and not renewed
* `eba_CO:x5` - Termination for cause: Provider in breach of applicable law, regulations or contractual provisions
* `eba_CO:x6` - Termination for cause: Identified impediments of the provider capable of altering the supported function
* `eba_CO:x7` - Termination for cause: Provider's weaknesses regarding the management and security of sensitive data or information
* `eba_CO:x8` - Termination: As requested by the competent authority
* `eba_CO:x9` - Other reasons for termination

Method used to quote the study: 'manual' for manual likelihood assessment, 'express' for automatic propagation from operating modes

* `manual` - Manual
* `express` - Express

version.description: Version of the Ebios RM study (eg. 1.0, 2.0, etc.)

quotation_method.description:

assets.description: Assets that are pertinent to the study

compliance_assessments.description: Compliance assessments established as security baseline during workshop 1.4

* `S` - Small

* `L` - Large
* `XL` - Extra Large

attack_stage.description:
Stage of the attack in the kill chain (e.g., 'Know', 'Enter', 'Discover', 'Exploit')

* `0` - ebiosReconnaissance
* `1` - ebiosInitialAccess
* `2` - ebiosDiscovery
* `3` - ebiosExploitation

PatchedAuditSink.description:
Serializer for audit-sink endpoints (kind=AUDIT_SINK): admin-managed
destinations that forward the audit log to an external SIEM. No HMAC secret
or per-event subscription — the whole audit feed is forwarded in the chosen
body_format, authenticated via static headers.

* `http` - HTTP
* `kafka` - Kafka

body_format.description:

* `ciso_native` - CISO Assistant (HMAC-signed)
* `ocsf` - OCSF
* `raw` - Raw LogEntry

summary_until_ts.description: Watermark — created_at of the most recent message folded into the rolling summary. Null = nothing folded yet.

version.description: Version of the compliance assessment (eg. 1.0, 2.0, etc.)

field_visibility.description: Per-field visibility map: {field_name: {role: 'edit' | 'read' | 'hidden'}}. Missing keys cascade through core.utils.DEFAULT_VISIBILITY (e.g. score/documentation_score default to hidden, status/extended_result to auditor-only) and finally to 'edit' for every role for unknown fields.

annual_expense.description: Annual expense amount for this contract

termination_reason.description:
Reason for contract termination

* `eba_CO:x4` - Termination not for cause: Expired and not renewed
* `eba_CO:x5` - Termination for cause: Provider in breach of applicable law, regulations or contractual provisions
* `eba_CO:x6` - Termination for cause: Identified impediments of the provider capable of altering the supported function
* `eba_CO:x7` - Termination for cause: Provider's weaknesses regarding the management and security of sensitive data or information
* `eba_CO:x8` - Termination: As requested by the competent authority
* `eba_CO:x9` - Other reasons for termination

overarching_contract.description: Parent/overarching contract if this is a subordinate arrangement

timestamp.description: When the metric sample was recorded

value.description: The metric value (format depends on metric definition category)

target_object_id.description: For builtin metrics: the ID of the specific object

metric_key.description: For builtin metrics: which metric to display (e.g., 'progress', 'result_breakdown')

title.description: Custom title for the widget. If empty, uses metric instance name or metric key.

show_target.description: Display target value line if defined on metric instance

show_legend.title: Show title and legend
show_legend.description: Display widget title bar and legend (for charts)

metric_instance.description: For custom metrics: the metric instance to display

target_content_type.description: For builtin metrics: the type of object (e.g., ComplianceAssessment)

affected_subjects_count.description: Approximate number of affected data subjects

affected_personal_data_count.description: Approximate number of affected personal data

incident.description: Link to associated security incident investigation

authorities.description: Regulatory authorities to notify (e.g., CNIL, ICO, etc.)

version.description: Version of the Ebios RM study (eg. 1.0, 2.0, etc.)

quotation_method.description:
Method used to quote the study: 'manual' for manual likelihood assessment, 'express' for automatic propagation from operating modes

* `manual` - Manual
* `express` - Express

reference_entity.description: Entity that is the focus of the study

assets.description: Assets that are pertinent to the study

compliance_assessments.description: Compliance assessments established as security baseline during workshop 1.4

attack_stage.description:
Stage of the attack in the kill chain (e.g., 'Know', 'Enter', 'Discover', 'Exploit')

* `0` - ebiosReconnaissance
* `1` - ebiosInitialAccess
* `2` - ebiosDiscovery
* `3` - ebiosExploitation

dora_assets_value.description: Total assets value for DORA reporting

dora_competent_authority.description: Competent authority overseeing this entity for DORA compliance

relationship.description: Type of relationship with this entity

attachment_hash.title: Attachment SHA256 Hash
attachment_hash.description: SHA256 hash of the attachment file for integrity verification

assets.description: Assets that are affected by the feared event

PatchedFeatureFlags.description:
Serializer for managing Feature Flags stored within the 'value' JSON field
of a GlobalSettings instance. Each flag is represented as an explicit
BooleanField, mapping directly to keys within the 'value' dictionary.

version.description: Version of the compliance assessment (eg. 1.0, 2.0, etc.)

create_iam_groups.description: Automatically provision IAM groups for domain folders.

field_visibility.description: Per-field visibility template seeded into new CAs: {field_name: {role: 'edit' | 'read' | 'hidden'}}.

urn_namespace.description: Organization identifier used in the URN prefix.

PatchedIntegrationConfiguration.description: Serializer for creating, reading, and updating IntegrationConfiguration instances.

webhook_url_full.description: Construct the full, absolute webhook URL

logic_operator.description:
Logic operator to apply between antecedents

* `AND` - AND
* `OR` - OR

position_x.description: X position of the node in the graph editor

position_y.description: Y position of the node in the graph editor

antecedents.description: Elementary actions that are antecedents to this action in the kill chain

choices_definition.description: For qualitative metrics: ordered list of options with translations. Format: [{'name': 'Low', 'description': '', 'translations': {'fr': {'name': 'Faible', 'description': ''}}}]

higher_is_better.description: If true, an increase in value is considered positive (e.g., compliance score). If false, a decrease is considered positive (e.g., number of vulnerabilities).

default_target.description: Default target value for metric instances. Can be overridden at instance level.

unit.description: Unit of measurement (e.g., count, users, bytes, percentage, score, event_per_second)

target_value.description: Target or optimal value for this metric instance

collection_frequency.description:
Expected frequency for collecting metric samples

* `realtime` - Real-time (continuous)
* `hourly` - Hourly
* `daily` - Daily
* `weekly` - Weekly
* `monthly` - Monthly
* `quarterly` - Quarterly
* `yearly` - Yearly

graph_columns.description: Stage column positions and sizes in the graph editor

operating_modes_description.description: Description of the operating modes of the operational scenario

threats.description: Threats leveraged by the operational scenario

expiry_date.description: Date after which the applied control is no longer valid

link.description: External url for action follow-up (eg. Jira ticket)

effort.description:
Relative effort of the measure (using T-Shirt sizing)

* `XS` - Extra Small
* `S` - Small
* `M` - Medium
* `L` - Large
* `XL` - Extra Large

assets.description: Assets impacted by the risk scenario

vulnerabilities.description: Vulnerabities exploited by the risk scenario

risk_tolerance.description: Risk tolerance points and curve data. Expected format: {'points': {'point1': {'probability': float, 'acceptable_loss': float}, 'point2': {'probability': float, 'acceptable_loss': float}}, 'curve_data': {'loss_values': [...], 'probability_values': [...]}}

portfolio_simulation.description: Cached portfolio simulation results to improve performance. Contains current and residual portfolio calculations with metadata.

ord.title: Order
ord.description: 0-based row index in the source sheet, used for export.

answer_candidates.description: Controlled vocabulary the customer expects in this question's answer cell, captured from the Excel data-validation list (or distinct existing values). Empty list = free-text cell.

answer_mapping.description: {yes, partial, no, source} mapping our internal status onto this question's vocabulary. Filled by suggest_value_mapping after extract; export uses it per-question (rather than the run-level value_mapping) so questionnaires with mixed vocabularies still produce a clean fill.

PatchedQuestionnaireRunWrite.description:
Generic CRUD writer. ``column_mapping`` / ``value_mapping`` go through
dedicated validated endpoints (set_mapping / suggest_value_mapping) — keep
them out of the writable surface here so a generic PATCH can't store
arbitrary blobs that the extract step then trusts.

visibility_expression.description: CEL expression that must evaluate to true for this requirement to be visible in an assessment.

description.description: Markdown-formatted long-form description of this activity

expiry_date.description: Specify when the risk acceptance will no longer apply

risk_scenarios.description: Select the risk scenarios to be accepted, attention they must be part of the chosen domain

version.description: Version of the compliance assessment (eg. 1.0, 2.0, etc.)

risk_matrix.description: WARNING! After choosing it, you will not be able to change it

is_enabled.title: Enabled
is_enabled.description: If the risk matrix is set as disabled, it will not be available for selection for new risk assessments.

existing_controls.description: The existing controls to manage this risk. Edit the risk scenario to add extra applied controls.

inherent_level.description: The risk level if no measures are applied. Automatically updated on Save, based on the chosen risk matrix

current_level.description: The risk level given the current measures. Automatically updated on Save, based on the chosen risk matrix

residual_level.description: The risk level when all the extra measures are done. Automatically updated on Save, based on the chosen risk matrix

strength_of_knowledge.description: The strength of the knowledge supporting the assessment

operational_scenario.description: EBIOS RM operational scenario that generated this risk scenario

assets.description: Assets impacted by the risk scenario

vulnerabilities.description: Vulnerabities exploited by the risk scenario

incidents.description: Incidents that materialized this risk scenario

antecedent_scenarios.description: Risk scenarios that precede this scenario

PatchedSecIntelFeeds.description:
Serializer for managing Security Intelligence feed settings.
Controls which external feeds are enabled and network parameters.

expiration_date.description: Specify when the security exception will no longer apply

value_mapping.description: {yes, partial, no, needs_info, source} — maps internal status labels onto the customer's vocabulary, plus a 'source' tag ('data_validation' | 'distinct_values' | 'fallback'). Computed asynchronously after column mapping is saved; export uses it when present.

QuestionnaireRunWrite.description:
Generic CRUD writer. ``column_mapping`` / ``value_mapping`` go through
dedicated validated endpoints (set_mapping / suggest_value_mapping) — keep
them out of the writable surface here so a generic PATCH can't store
arbitrary blobs that the extract step then trusts.

dora_data_sensitiveness.title: Data sensitiveness
dora_data_sensitiveness.description:
Sensitiveness of the data stored

* `eba_ZZ:x791` - Low
* `eba_ZZ:x792` - Medium
* `eba_ZZ:x793` - High

dora_reliance_level.title: Level of reliance
dora_reliance_level.description:
Level of reliance on the ICT service

* `eba_ZZ:x794` - Not significant
* `eba_ZZ:x795` - Low reliance
* `eba_ZZ:x796` - Material reliance
* `eba_ZZ:x797` - Full reliance

dora_substitutability.title: Substitutability
dora_substitutability.description:
Substitutability of the ICT third-party service provider

* `eba_ZZ:x959` - Not substitutable
* `eba_ZZ:x962` - Easily substitutable
* `eba_ZZ:x961` - Medium complexity in terms of substitutability
* `eba_ZZ:x960` - Highly complex substitutability

dora_non_substitutability_reason.title: Non-substitutability reason
dora_non_substitutability_reason.description:
Reason if the ICT third-party service provider is considered not substitutable or difficult to be substitutable

* `eba_ZZ:x963` - Lack of real alternatives
* `eba_ZZ:x964` - Difficulties in migrating or reintegrating
* `eba_ZZ:x965` - Lack of real alternatives and difficulties in migrating or reintegrating

dora_has_exit_plan.title: Exit plan
dora_has_exit_plan.description:
Existence of an exit plan

* `eba_BT:x28` - Yes
* `eba_BT:x29` - No

dora_reintegration_possibility.title: Reintegration possibility
dora_reintegration_possibility.description:
Possibility of reintegration of the contracted ICT service

* `eba_ZZ:x798` - Easy
* `eba_ZZ:x966` - Difficult
* `eba_ZZ:x967` - Highly complex
* `eba_ZZ:x0` - Not applicable

dora_discontinuing_impact.title: Discontinuing impact
dora_discontinuing_impact.description:
Impact of discontinuing the ICT services

* `eba_ZZ:x791` - Low
* `eba_ZZ:x792` - Medium
* `eba_ZZ:x793` - High
* `eba_ZZ:x799` - Assessment not performed

dora_alternative_providers_identified.title: Alternative providers identified
dora_alternative_providers_identified.description:
Are there alternative ICT third-party service providers identified?

* `eba_BT:x28` - Yes
* `eba_BT:x29` - No
* `eba_BT:x21` - Assessment not performed

dora_alternative_providers.title: Alternative providers
dora_alternative_providers.description: Identification of alternative ICT third-party service providers

SolutionSubcontractorWrite.description:
Write shape for nested chain rows. Deliberately NOT a ModelSerializer —
`solution` is set by the parent SolutionWriteSerializer from URL context,
not accepted from the client. `id` is also ignored; the chain is fully
replaced on each PATCH.

`recipient` is optional — null means "direct provider" (the common case
for fan-out entries directly under the provider).

dora_ict_service_type.description:
DORA ICT service type classification

* `eba_TA:S01` - ICT project management
* `eba_TA:S02` - ICT Development
* `eba_TA:S03` - ICT help desk and first level support
* `eba_TA:S04` - ICT security management services
* `eba_TA:S05` - Provision of data
* `eba_TA:S06` - Data analysis
* `eba_TA:S07` - ICT, facilities and hosting services (excluding Cloud services)
* `eba_TA:S08` - Computation
* `eba_TA:S09` - Non-Cloud Data storage
* `eba_TA:S10` - Telecom carrier
* `eba_TA:S11` - Network infrastructure
* `eba_TA:S12` - Hardware and physical devices
* `eba_TA:S13` - Software licencing (excluding SaaS)
* `eba_TA:S14` - ICT operation management (including maintenance)
* `eba_TA:S15` - ICT Consulting
* `eba_TA:S16` - ICT Risk management
* `eba_TA:S17` - Cloud services: IaaS
* `eba_TA:S18` - Cloud services: PaaS
* `eba_TA:S19` - Cloud services: SaaS
